description.content: Submits an SQL query string for execution. Returns an initial QueryResults response which may contain the first batch of results and/or a nextUri for polling subsequent results. Clients must include the X-Trino-User header at minimum. Additional context headers (catalog, schema, session properties) may be provided to configure the query environment.

header.description: (Required) The user identity submitting the query
Default catalog for the query session
Default schema for the query session
Identifies the client application submitting the query
Comma-separated list of session property assignments in key=value format
Transaction identifier for query grouping. Use NONE for auto-commit.
Comma-separated client tags for resource group matching
Arbitrary client-supplied metadata about the client
Time zone for the query session (IANA tz database format)
BCP 47 language tag for locale-sensitive operations
External trace token for distributed tracing correlation
Connector-specific extra credentials in name=value format. May appear multiple times.

response.name: Query accepted and initial results returned

header.description: (Required) The user identity submitting the query
Default catalog for the query session
Default schema for the query session
Identifies the client application submitting the query
Comma-separated list of session property assignments in key=value format
Transaction identifier for query grouping. Use NONE for auto-commit.
Comma-separated client tags for resource group matching
Arbitrary client-supplied metadata about the client
Time zone for the query session (IANA tz database format)
BCP 47 language tag for locale-sensitive operations
External trace token for distributed tracing correlation
Connector-specific extra credentials in name=value format. May appear multiple times.
New default catalog set by the query (USE CATALOG statement)
New default schema set by the query (USE SCHEMA statement)
Session property set by the query
Session property cleared by the query
Transaction ID started by a START TRANSACTION statement
Indicates the transaction was committed or rolled back

response.name: Bad request — malformed SQL or invalid session parameters

header.description: (Required) The user identity submitting the query
Default catalog for the query session
Default schema for the query session
Identifies the client application submitting the query

Time zone for the query session (IANA tz database format)
BCP 47 language tag for locale-sensitive operations
External trace token for distributed tracing correlation
Connector-specific extra credentials in name=value format. May appear multiple times.

response.name: Too many requests. Client must wait the duration specified in Retry-After header before retrying.

header.description: (Required) The user identity submitting the query
Default catalog for the query session
Default schema for the query session
Identifies the client application submitting the query
Comma-separated list of session property assignments in key=value format
Transaction identifier for query grouping. Use NONE for auto-commit.
Comma-separated client tags for resource group matching
Arbitrary client-supplied metadata about the client
Time zone for the query session (IANA tz database format)
BCP 47 language tag for locale-sensitive operations
External trace token for distributed tracing correlation
Connector-specific extra credentials in name=value format. May appear multiple times.
Number of seconds to wait before retrying

response.name: Service unavailable — transient cluster error; retry after 50-100ms

header.description: (Required) The user identity submitting the query
Default catalog for the query session
Default schema for the query session
Identifies the client application submitting the query

description.content: Retrieves the next batch of results for a running query. Clients should follow the nextUri from a previous QueryResults response. When no nextUri is present in the response, the query is complete.

query.description: Maximum duration to wait for results before returning empty batch

variable.description: (Required) The unique query identifier returned by POST /v1/statement
(Required) Sequential token for paging through results

header.description: (Required) The user identity (must match original query submission)

response.name: Next batch of results (may be empty if still processing)

variable.description: (Required) The unique query identifier returned by POST /v1/statement
(Required) Sequential token for paging through results

header.description: (Required) The user identity (must match original query submission)

response.name: Query not found — expired or already cancelled

variable.description: (Required) The unique query identifier returned by POST /v1/statement
(Required) Sequential token for paging through results

header.description: (Required) The user identity (must match original query submission)

response.name: Too many concurrent polls; wait and retry

variable.description: (Required) The unique query identifier returned by POST /v1/statement
(Required) Sequential token for paging through results

header.description: (Required) The user identity (must match original query submission)

description.content: Cancels a running query. Clients should DELETE the nextUri returned by the most recent QueryResults response to abort execution.

variable.description: (Required) Sequential token matching the current position

header.description: (Required) The user identity

variable.description: (Required) Sequential token matching the current position

header.description: (Required) The user identity

variable.description: (Required) Sequential token matching the current position

header.description: (Required) The user identity

description.content: Returns general information about the Trino cluster including version, uptime, and whether the coordinator is still starting up. Used by load balancers and Trino Gateway to verify cluster health.
Returns a list of all active worker nodes in the Trino cluster, including their node ID, URI, last heartbeat time, and resource usage.

The Trino Client REST API allows clients to submit SQL queries to a Trino coordinator and retrieve results. The protocol is HTTP-based: clients POST a query to /v1/statement, then poll GET on the returned nextUri until results are fully retrieved, and may DELETE nextUri to cancel a running query. Trino is a distributed SQL query engine for big data analytics supporting connectors to data lakes, relational databases, and NoSQL stores.

Contact Support:
 Name: Trino Community
